audit_hypothesis: dockerfile_commit may equal commit_sha (the FIX commit), making the buggy state actually the fixed state
audit_verdict: HYPOTHESIS REJECTED FOR ALL 3 TASKS — dockerfile commits are correctly the PARENT of the fix commit
method: Read environment/Dockerfile for each task; extracted git checkout SHA -> dockerfile_commit
Read tests/install_config.json for each task; extracted commit_sha (the FIX commit)
Read data-pipeline/artifacts_cli/canonical_patches/<sid>.json; extracted _base_commit (the parent of the fix)
Cross-validated upstream by cloning github.com/entireio/cli and running 'git rev-parse <fix_sha>^' for each
Confirmed git diff --stat <dockerfile_commit> <commit_sha> shows non-trivial changes touching declared source_files

cli-task-0ec2e9.notes: Dockerfile correctly checks out the parent of the FIX commit. No correction required.

summary.rationale_for_no_baseline_run: No bug was confirmed in any of the 3 tasks; baseline validation was unnecessary. All Dockerfiles correctly synthesize the buggy (pre-fix) state by cloning at the upstream parent of the FIX commit.
